title: Piquant Peruvian Pollo a la Brasa
description: A resinous, molten Peruvian chicken dish with a sweet icing sugar glaze and savory dashi‑infused sauce, perfect for a low‑glycemic dinner.
instructions: Combine olive oil, dashi powder, icing sugar, soy sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper in a bowl to make the marinating sauce.
Pat the chicken thighs dry and place them in a shallow dish, pouring the sauce over the meat and turning to coat evenly.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours, to allow the flavors to meld.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 375°F (190°C) for an even char.
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 10 minutes before grilling.
Grill the thighs for about 6 to 7 minutes per side, basting with leftover sauce, until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the surface is charred.
Transfer the cooked chicken to a platter and let it rest for 5 minutes to retain juices.
Serve the pollo a la brasa hot, optionally garnished with a sprinkle of fresh cilantro or a squeeze of lime.
